thinking:
**Observing the Interface**

I've got a grasp of the screen's layout now. The user's feedback, "I didn't like the design, make it a more beautiful design," has shifted my thinking. I'm focusing on aesthetic improvements and alternative design approaches. I'm starting to visualize how I can refine the interface to meet their preferences.


**Designing a Wow-Factor**

I'm now diving deep into creating a premium, state-of-the-art monthly prayer times screen. My new implementation plan centers on a dual-view concept with a glowing tab selector. I'm prioritizing user choice through modern list and spiritual calendar grid views. The calendar view will use elegant, rounded cards, and circular badges. "Today" is highlighted with an emerald glow. I believe this design will truly wow the user!


**Refining the Card List**

I'm now zeroing in on a modern card-based list view. I'm moving away from a dense table to horizontal premium cards, aiming for a sleek look. I can also build a highly-polished table with custom dividers. I'm incorporating scrollable functionality with micro-animations. Each card will feature a date icon on the left. The prayer times will be arranged in a horizontal layout. We're thinking gold and emerald color harmony, with elevated "Today" cards using circular dial badges with custom gold border styling and glowing background.


**Implementing New Displays**

I'm now implementing the redesigned monthly prayer times screen with a dual-view system! I've added a state variable for an interactive display toggle, enabling users to switch between list and calendar views. I'm building a glassmorphic toggle bar with icons for "Liste Görünümü" and "Takvim Görünümü". The "Günün Vakitleri" header now displays today's times in a horizontal scroll of capsule cards. Below, I'm refactoring the monthly view into an elegant grid.


**Enhancing the List View**

I'm now implementing the refined list view, which will feature a "Today's Featured Card" with gold embellishments and emerald backgrounds, prominently displaying today's times with rounded badges. Below, I'm designing an elegant grid-like table, optimizing spacing and incorporating custom color accents for each prayer column, with corresponding icons in each header. Each column header will look like a luxury dashboard!

args.toolAction: "Viewing lines 30-75 of tool_detail_screen.dart to see state variable declarations"
args.toolSummary: "View state variables in tool detail screen"
